commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh A haughty look, a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin. Or when it is proud, it causes the eyes to appear so too. (Calmet) Lamp. Protestants, "ploughing of the wicked is sin. "When it is done with a bad motive, out of pride. Hebrew, "the haughtiness of the eyes, and the enlarging of the heart, are the labour ("tillage "Montanus; "thought "Pagnin) of the wicked; yea, sin. "(Haydock) Those things are their employment, and their sin. (Calmet) Septuagint and Vulgate may also mean, "sin is the lamp and exultation of the wicked.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
